up.method: carbon source for modified Wood-Ljungdahl pathway; no rate reported

sec.method: metabolic by-product of hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis; no rate reported

growth_records.cite: Thevasundaram et al. Engineering nonphotosynthetic carbon fixation for production of bioplastics by methanogenic archaea. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2118638119. [PMC9191640]
growth_records.snippet: relatively fast doubling time for methanogens (~2 h)... M. maripaludis uses CO2 and H2 as carbon and electron sources, respectively, producing acetyl-CoA as an intermediate for biosynthesis and methane.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from the paper's stated approximate doubling time: the paper describes M. maripaludis S2 as having a 'relatively fast doubling time for methanogens (~2 h)'. mu = ln2/td = 0.693/2.0 h = 0.347/h, rounded 0.35/h. This ~2 h value is a general characterization (Introduction), not a per-condition measurement, hence low confidence. Medium McCas is a rich undefined medium; full composition NOT given in this paper (cited to Tumbula & Whitman 1999, ref 37) so medium_composition_stated=false; only nitrogen sources (NH4Cl + Ala) and casamino acids/vitamins are indicated. Carbon source = CO2, electron donor = H2 (no uptake fluxes reported). Methane is the by-product (no secretion rate reported). O2: obligate anaerobe.

growth_records.medium: McNA-Ala (minimal defined medium with L-alanine as sole nitrogen source)

up.method: 10 mM supplied as sole nitrogen source; oxidatively deaminated by Ald to pyruvate + NH4+; ~4% of product carbon labeled from 13C-Ala; no uptake flux reported

sec.method: reported; HPLC-MS/MS; 16.3 +/- 2.7 mg/L, highest of the three media (McCas, McNA, McNA-Ala)

growth_records.cite: Thevasundaram et al. Engineering nonphotosynthetic carbon fixation for production of bioplastics by methanogenic archaea. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2118638119. [PMC9191640]
growth_records.snippet: we found that growth in McNA-Ala medium led to S-3HB titers of 16.3 +/- 2.7 mg/L, the highest titer of the three media conditions... McNA-Ala, minimal defined medium with 10 mM Ala.
growth_records.notes: S-3HB titer 16.3 +/- 2.7 mg/L reported (product TITER, not a specific secretion flux; no biomass or time-resolved data to compute qP in mmol/gDW/h). Medium McNA-Ala = minimal defined medium with 10 mM L-alanine as sole N source; full mineral base composition NOT given here (cited refs), so medium_composition_stated=false and only the stated 10 mM Ala is listed. No growth rate measured for this condition. Carbon from CO2, electrons from H2 (hydrogenotrophic); no substrate uptake fluxes reported. 37 C, static, 7-day production.

growth_records.strain: S2, engineered PhaABC (PhaA C.acetobutylicum + PhaB H.bluephagenesis + PhaC C.necator) + NadMV salvage + Fdh1 (C.boidinii formate dehydrogenase)
growth_records.medium: McNA (minimal defined medium with 10 mM ammonium chloride)

up.method: carbon source via modified Wood-Ljungdahl pathway; CO2 from formate oxidation also re-fixed; no rate reported
alternative energy source; oxidized by heterologous Fdh1 to regenerate NADH + CO2; no uptake flux reported

growth_records.cite: Thevasundaram et al. Engineering nonphotosynthetic carbon fixation for production of bioplastics by methanogenic archaea. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2118638119. [PMC9191640]
growth_records.snippet: final titers of 158 +/- 6 mg/L (R-3HB) and 171 +/- 4 mg/L, respectively... These yields correspond to 26.3 +/- 1.6% and 24.0 +/- 1.9%, respectively, of dry cell weight.
growth_records.notes: PHB titer 171 +/- 4 mg/L and yield 24.0 +/- 1.9% of dry cell weight reported directly (product-per-biomass yield; Ypx = 0.240 g PHB/gDCW). These are TITER and biomass-fraction yields, not specific fluxes; no substrate uptake or time-resolved biomass data given to compute qP or qS in mmol/gDW/h. Medium McNA = minimal defined + 10 mM NH4Cl (full base composition cited to refs, not given here) so medium_composition_stated=false; supplemented with 50 mM nicotinamide (NadMV salvage) and formate (Fdh1). Optimized growth: 30 C, 250 rpm, H2/CO2 80/20 to 275 kPa daily, 14 d. No per-condition growth rate reported (organism general doubling ~2 h noted only in Introduction). Obligate anaerobe.

growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (M. maripaludis monoculture, constant shaking); co-culture agitation not separately specified
growth_records.medium: B3n medium (B3m without PIPES) amended with acetate, yeast extract, cysteine and sulfide

up.method: experimental, Table 3 (measured over 25 h batch on H2/CO2/acetate)

growth_records.cite: Stolyar et al. Metabolic modeling of a mutualistic microbial community. Molecular Systems Biology (2007). DOI:10.1038/msb4100131. [PMC1847946]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: Metabolite Experimental data — Hydrogen uptake 0.65, Acetate uptake 0.006, Carbon dioxide uptake 0.21, Methane production 0.2 (mmol/h); Biomass production 0.294 mg DCW/h.
growth_records.notes: M. maripaludis S2 monoculture, this paper's own measurement over 25 h batch on H2+CO2+acetate. Table 3 experimental column: H2 uptake 0.65, acetate uptake 0.006, CO2 uptake 0.21, CH4 production 0.2 mmol/h; biomass production 0.294 mg DCW/h (whole-culture/bottle rates, not per gDW). Temperature not stated for this specific monoculture growth experiment (routine culturing was 37 C, 200 rpm); left null. pH 7.2 taken from B3m base medium. Base medium B3n = B3m minus Na2-PIPES; monoculture amended with 10 mM Na acetate, 2 g/L yeast extract, elevated NaCl 4 g/L, 3 mM cysteine, 0.5 g/L sodium sulfide (Methods 'Experimental determination of growth and metabolic activity'). No mu/doubling derivable: only biomass production rate (mg DCW/h) given, standing biomass not stated, so mu=ln2/td cannot be computed. Thauer's vitamins per L stock: biotin 0.02 g, folic acid 0.02 g, pyridoxine HCl 0.1 g, thiamine HCl 0.05 g, riboflavin 0.05 g, nicotinic acid 0.05 g, DL-pantothenate 0.05 g, p-aminobenzoic acid 0.05 g, vitamin B12 0.01 g. Trace minerals per L stock: FeCl2.4H2O 1.0 g, MnCl2.4H2O 0.5 g, CoCl2.4H2O 0.3 g, ZnCl2 0.2 g, Na2MoO4.4H2O 0.05 g, H3BO3 0.02 g, NiSO4.6H2O 0.1 g, CuCl2.2H2O 0.002 g, Na2SeO3.5H2O 0.006 g, Na2WO4.2H2O 0.008 g. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

growth_records.medium: Modified coculture medium (CCM) with acetate replacing lactate

up.method: Growth-limiting energy substrate (H2 limitation); specific rate not reported and not derivable (no biomass gDW, no gas balance given for monoculture)
Carbon/energy substrate for hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis; rate not reported
30 mM acetate supplied for carbon assimilation; specific uptake rate not reported

sec.method: Methanogenesis product; specific rate not reported for monoculture

growth_records.cite: Walker et al. Functional responses of methanogenic archaea to syntrophic growth. ISME J (2012). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2012.60. [PMC3475374]
growth_records.snippet: M. maripaludis monocultures were grown in Bioflo 110 bioreactors... on modified CCM containing 30 mM acetate instead of lactate. Sodium sulfide was replaced in the medium with sparging... of a 1% H2S:N2 mixture... All the transcription data
growth_records.notes: H2-limited chemostat monoculture run at the SAME generation time as the coculture (stated), so mu = D = 0.039 h-1 and doubling time = ln2/0.039 = 17.7 h (inherited from the coculture retention time of 25.6 h). Energy substrate = H2 (limiting) + CO2; acetate (30 mM) supplied only for carbon assimilation (M. maripaludis is autotrophic/acetate-assimilating). No biomass dry weight and no monoculture-specific gas balance reported, so no specific uptake/secretion fluxes derivable. CCM composition cited to Walker et al. 2009, not in this paper -> medium_composition_stated=false.

up.units: mL/min (gas supply flow to chemostat, H2-excess; not a biomass-specific rate)

sec.units: not numerically stated in text (rates measured for all cultures; values shown only in Fig. 1)

growth_records.cite: Yoon et al. A systems level predictive model for global gene regulation of methanogenesis in a hydrogenotrophic methanogen. Genome Research (2013). DOI:10.1101/gr.153916.112. [PMC3814884]
growth_records.snippet: The dilution rate was held constant at 0.083 h-1. For time-series array data, cultures before perturbation were allowed to reach steady state... The standard gassing regime was a 110-mL/min H2, 40-mL/min CO2, 35-mL/min Ar, and 15-mL/min H2S
growth_records.notes: At chemostat steady state mu = dilution rate D. D explicitly stated = 0.083 h-1, so mu = 0.083 h-1 (growth_method=calculated via mu=D). Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.083 = 8.35 h. Steady-state cell density OD660 between 0.6 and 0.8 (text). Medium only partially specified in-text: standard PO4 0.8 mM (limited 0.12 mM WT / 0.18 mM for dMMP1100), NH4+ 10 mM (limited 2.8 mM); full mineral-medium recipe not given (cited). H2/CO2 supplied as gas flow (110 mL/min H2 excess, 21 mL/min limiting; 40 mL/min CO2) — these are supply flows, not biomass-specific uptake rates, so no qS in mmol/gDW/h derivable. CH4 production measured for all cultures but numeric rates are only in Fig. 1 (not in text). Same D=0.083 h-1 chemostat applied to WT MM901 and to deletion strains (dmtd, dfruAdfrcA, dMMP1447, dMMP0719, dMMP1100); growth rate identical (=D) across strains, hence single record. Temperature and pH not stated in text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

growth_records.cite: Lei Yue et al. The conserved ribonuclease aCPSF1 triggers genome-wide transcription termination of Archaea via a 3'-end cleavage mode. Nucleic Acids Research (2020). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaa702. [PMC7515710]
growth_records.snippet: ▽aCPSF1 exhibited markedly reduced growth at 22°C (μ = 0.035 h−1 versus 0.06 h−1 of S2, Figure 1B)
growth_records.notes: Measured specific growth rate μ=0.06 h−1 for wild-type strain S2 at 22°C, read from growth curves (Figure 1B). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/μ = 0.693147/0.06 = 11.55 h. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates were measured in this paper (transcription-termination study). Medium is McF (composition cited from ref 28, not stored in-text) so medium_composition left empty. pH not stated. M. maripaludis is a hydrogenotrophic methanogen (H2/CO2 or formate) but substrate and methane rates were not quantified here. At 37°C S2 had a 'similar growth rate' to the mutant but no numeric μ was given (Supplementary Figure S1C), so no 37°C record was emitted.

growth_records.cite: Lei Yue et al. The conserved ribonuclease aCPSF1 triggers genome-wide transcription termination of Archaea via a 3'-end cleavage mode. Nucleic Acids Research (2020). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaa702. [PMC7515710]
growth_records.snippet: ▽aCPSF1 exhibited markedly reduced growth at 22°C (μ = 0.035 h−1 versus 0.06 h−1 of S2, Figure 1B)
growth_records.notes: Measured μ=0.035 h−1 for the Mmp-aCPSF1 expression-depleted strain at 22°C (Figure 1B). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/μ = 0.693147/0.035 = 19.80 h. This is an engineered depletion strain (indigenous Mmp-aCPSF1 knocked out, expression driven by a repressible tetO system; ~3-fold lower aCPSF1 protein than 37°C culture), included because μ was directly measured; note reduced growth is a genetic-perturbation phenotype, not wild-type physiology. A ~2-fold longer bulk mRNA half-life (8.4 min vs 4.7 min for S2) was also reported but is not a growth-physiology rate. No uptake/secretion/MFA fluxes measured.
